_comment:
Texts in English — tooltips (tip_*) and Conventions dialog (conv_*). Edit the text freely. The conv_*_explain and conv_intro texts accept simple HTML (

, , ). Do NOT rename the keys.
conv_intro:
These conventions affect some computed values (distance, rise/set times). Default values are optimized for EME

General astronomy software (e.g. mooncalc.org, Stellarium) often uses different conventions because it targets visual moon observation. For a radio link calculation, EME conventions are more accurate.
conv_dist_explain:
Topocentric: actual distance between your antenna and the Moon. This is the distance the radio signal travels.

Geocentric: distance from Earth's center (classical astronomical convention, used by mooncalc).

The difference can reach ~6,378 km (Earth radius) when Moon is at zenith. For EME, topocentric distance is essential as it determines path loss and signal delay.
conv_hor_explain:
Geometric: the Moon is considered risen/set when its center crosses the geometric horizon (0°). This is the reference for EME link budget — below 0° the Moon is blocked by the Earth.

Visual: classical astronomical convention. The Moon is "risen" when its upper limb becomes visible to the observer (semi-diameter ~15' + atmospheric refraction ~34' = 0.83° below geometric horizon). This is what mooncalc shows.

For EME, atmospheric refraction is negligible for radio signals (much less than optical), and the Moon's "limb" has no meaning for a radar echo. Geometric horizon is therefore the correct choice.
tip_save: Save callsign, locator, altitude and preferences.
tip_row_dblclick:
Click a pass row to see the detail
every 30 minutes (AZ, EL, Doppler, DGR, TSky...).
Click the NOW row to open the real-time dashboard
with all EME parameters.
tip_sked:
Online EME QSO planning tool
by SA5IKN — skeds, mutual visibility, etc.
tip_phase_chk:
Show lunar phase (visual only,
no effect on radio EME)
tip_local_time: Display in local time instead of UTC
tip_quality_filter:
Filter passes by minimum EME quality index
(0 to 8)
tip_duration:
Total pass duration above the horizon
Green ≥ 5h, orange ≥ 2h, red < 2h
tip_el_max:
MAXIMUM ELEVATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Maximum height of the Moon in the sky
during this pass.

  Green  ≥ 20° : short atmospheric path, low loss
  Orange ≥ 10° : acceptable
  Red    < 10° : high atmospheric loss,
                  high ground noise

At 10 GHz, atmospheric absorption increases
rapidly below 10° elevation.
tip_el_max_time: Time when the Moon reaches maximum elevation
tip_decl:
LUNAR DECLINATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Angle of the Moon relative to the celestial equatorial plane.

  Positive (+) = Moon north of the equator
  Negative (−) = Moon south of the equator

Affects the maximum height of the Moon:
  High decl. + northern latitude → higher max EL
  Ranges from −28.6° to +28.6° over 18.6 years.
tip_distance:
EARTH-MOON DISTANCE (km)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Topocentric distance at maximum elevation.

  Perigee (min.) : ~356,500 km
  Apogee (max.)  : ~406,700 km
  Average         : ~384,400 km

Skyfield + JPL DE440s calculations (sub-km accuracy).
tip_extra_pl:
EXTRA PATH LOSS — Additional loss vs perigee
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Extra PL = 40 × log₁₀(d / 356,500)

  +0.0 dB = perigee (optimal)
  +5.8 dB = apogee (worst case)

Factor 40: round-trip path (d⁴).
tip_total_pl:
TOTAL PATH LOSS — Complete EME attenuation
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Total = PL perigee + Extra PL

Change frequency to recalculate.
tip_moon_sun:
MOON-SUN ANGLE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  180° = opposition (no solar noise)
  0°   = conjunction (max solar noise)

  > 15° : no degradation
  5-15° : possible S/N degradation
  < 5°  : very difficult EME
tip_libration:
LIBRATION RATE (deg/h)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  < 0.10 deg/h : EXCELLENT
  0.10-0.25 : GOOD
  0.25-0.40 : MEDIUM
  > 0.40 : POOR

At 10 GHz, a high rate spreads the
reflected signal (Doppler spread).
At 144/432 MHz, the effect is negligible.
tip_doppler:
DOPPLER SPREAD EME (Hz)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Frequency spread of the reflected signal
due to libration.

  < 50 Hz  : excellent
  50-150 Hz : good
  > 150 Hz  : difficult
tip_spread_min:
SPREAD MIN — Best moment of the pass
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Minimum Doppler spread value over the
entire pass duration, with the time at which
the minimum occurs (the 'magic window').

Spread can vary by a factor of 3× between
rise, culmination and set, depending on the
geometry of the day (Moon declination,
observer latitude).

  < 50 Hz  : excellent
  50-150 Hz : good
  > 150 Hz  : difficult

Sampled at 30 points across the pass.
tip_spread_max:
SPREAD MAX — Worst moment of the pass
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Maximum Doppler spread value over the
entire pass duration, with the time.

Often at rise or set when diurnal libration
(due to Earth rotation) is strongest.

Sampled at 30 points across the pass.
tip_quality:
EME QUALITY INDEX (0 to 10)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  Max elevation (30%/20%)
  Duration (25%/10%)
  Path loss (25%)
  Moon-Sun (20%/15%)
  Libration (0%/30% if ≥ 1 GHz)

Score adapts to frequency:
  < 1 GHz : libration ignored
  ≥ 1 GHz : libration = 30% of score
